item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery near Moscow following drone attacks | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine says it has struck a Russian oil refinery near Moscow in retaliation for a wave of drone attacks on Kyiv and other cities. At least seven people were killed in the Ukrainian capital when Russian drones targeted power and water infrastructure, as well as residential areas. The UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres has condemned the attacks as violations of international law.
Chapters: 0:00 Ukraine says it has struck Russia 2:59 Mark Cancian, Center for Strategic & International Studies
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1 #ukraine #strikes #moscow

item.title: Cliff Asness on how #AI changes quantitative investing #stockmarket #shorts
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, Clifford Asness, co-founder and CEO of AQR Capital Management, joins Tracy Alloway and Joe Weisenthal to talk about the history of quantitative investing, market efficiency and the emergence of AI/ML in his process http://apple.co/4lTgFT6
The Odd Lots podcast has been around for 10 years. Unfortunately, markets have gotten less rational over the same time frame. At least this is the contention of Cliff Asness, the co-founder and CEO of AQR Capital Management, a quantitative investing firm that's been around for nearly three decades. Asness' approach to investing is rooted in academic theory, having studied under the legendary Eugene Fama at the University of Chicago. In the world of social media and meme stocks, it's tough out there for the academically minded. And that's forced Cliff to adjust his approach over time. On this episode, we talk about the history of quantitative investing, market efficiency, and the emergence of AI/ML in his process. We also talk about the reality of investing other people's money, and the challenge of sticking with one's convictions at a time when temporary forces are working against you.

item.title: Are climate protesters having any affect on UN climate talks in Brazil? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
The UN climate talks in Brazil are facing increasing disruption from protesters. Thousands have joined a so-called "Great People's March" outside the conference venue. They are demanding real action -- rather than just words -- on global warming.
CORRECTIONS: 1:23 The name should be spelled 'Huanca', not 'Hwanca'.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Trump to sue BBC despite apology over edit
item.author: Minnie Stephenson
item.content:encoded: The BBC has apologised to Donald Trump, over the way one of his speeches was edited, and retracted the Panorama episode involved.But that wasn’t enough for the US president, who says he’s determined to launch a multi billion dollar lawsuit. It’s not the first time Mr Trump has sued a media outlet of course, but it’s not entirely clear whether an overseas legal case would succeed.

item.title: Climate protesters demand to be heard as they continue demonstrations at UN COP30 talks
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Climate protesters demand to be heard as they continue demonstrations at UN COP30 talks
Speaking about the effects of climate change in their home countries, activists advocated for youth representation in the UN negotiations.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/11/15/climate-protesters-demand-to-be-heard-as-they-continue-demonstrations-at-un-cop30-talks

item.title: Trump signals that US is likely to scale up military action against Venezuela | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Donald Trump has signalled that the US is likely to scale up military action against Venezuela targeting alleged drug smuggling. A US task force in the Caribbean is expanding rapidly, and has carried out 20 strikes on suspected drug trafficking boats in the region. Donald Trump has threatened to expand these attacks onto Venezuelan soil, accusing President Nicolas Maduro of running a drug cartel. On board Air Force One, reporters pressed Trump on his plans.
Chapters: 0:00 Trump signals more action against Venezuela 0:52 John Feeley, Former US Ambassador to Panama
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: At least nine people killed over past 24 hours across Ukraine in Russian attacks
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
At least nine people killed over past 24 hours across Ukraine in Russian attacks
Russia launched three ballistic missiles over Ukraine during overnight attacks from Friday into Saturday, as the death toll over the past 24 hours rose to nine, according to Ukrainian authorities.

item.title: Beyond AI: Inside the Global Quantum Computer Race
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Einstein doubted it. But nearly a century later, quantum mechanics is driving one of the biggest technology races of our time. From drug discovery to finance and cybersecurity, the quantum revolution may redefine how we compute, invest, and live, and it could even outperform AI. IBM’s Jamie Garcia and Jerry Chow explain why 2029 is the year to watch for quantum, while IonQ’s Niccolo de Masi says his company is already years ahead. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Jon Gray Is Reshaping Blackstone for the Retail Crowd
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
From viral jogging videos to advocating for new investment approaches, Blackstone President and COO Jon Gray is transforming a private equity giant built on exclusivity.
Blackstone, once a fortress for the world’s richest institutions, is remaking itself under Gray into a financial superstore for the dentist, lawyer and teacher next door. As Chief Executive Officer Steve Schwarzman steps back from much of the day-to-day management of the $1.2 trillion firm he built into a private equity giant, his heir apparent is driving a transformation focused on brand polish and popular appeal.
Read more: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2025-11-12/blackstone-s-jon-gray-opens-up-private-equity-to-the-retail-investing-masses

item.title: Massive attack of drones and missiles on Kyiv: What's Russia's strategy? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Six people have been killed in what Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has described as a "calculated" Russian attack on Kyiv. Dozens more were injured when the barrage of drones and missiles overwhelmed Ukraine's air defence systems in the early hours of Friday morning, targeting power and water infrastructure as well as residential areas.
00:00 Kyiv hit by barrage of Russian drones and missiles 02:56 DW speaks with Inna Sovsun, member of the Ukrainian Parliament and the Deputy Head of the Golos party from Kyiv 06:53 DW's Konstantin Eggert provides analysis
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Wall Street Week | Santander’s Ana Botín, Quantum Future, Pension Reform, Data Center Win-Win
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
This week, Santander’s Ana Botín is steering one of Europe’s biggest banks through red tape and rising taxes. She says that growth, not regulation, is what Europe needs most. And, quantum mechanics is driving a multibillion-dollar race. The technology is already in use, but measuring success is the next challenge for investors. Plus, the Netherlands is offering a blueprint for how pension systems around the world adapt. Later, can Finland’s plan to turn data center heat into clean power work worldwide?
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Santander's Ana Botín 00:11:06 - Quantum Computing Race 00:23:50 - Netherlands Pension Reform 00:35:17 - Finland Data Centers 00:47:19 - Quantum with IBM's Jerry Chow -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Deadly explosion rips through Kashmir police station | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Indian authorities say at least nine people have been killed and more than 30 injured in a blast at a police station. They say the detonation happened in the city of Srinagar in Indian-administered Kashmir. Police and forensic team members were handling confiscated explosives when the material accidentally blew up. It came just days after 13 people were killed in a blast in New Delhi. DW's New Delhi Bureau Chief Sandra Petersmann reports.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: US confirms deadly attack in the Caribbean: 80 dead under 'Operation Southern Spear'
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
US confirms deadly attack in the Caribbean: 80 dead under 'Operation Southern Spear'
The Trump Administration confirm a deadly attack in Caribbean waters that left four dead on 10 November, bringing to 80 the number of people killed in 'Operation Southern Spear'. The US president announces that he had “sort of made up" his "mind” on further military action against Venezuela.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/11/15/us-confirms-deadly-attack-in-the-caribbean-80-dead-under-operation-southern-spear

item.title: Heavy rains add to suffering of displaced Gazans in makeshift camps | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Displaced Palestinians in Gaza are having to contend with heavy autumn rains. The makeshift accomodation that many Gaza residents are living in have been flooded and they are facing another winter of incredibly difficult conditions.
00:00 Gazans struggle with heavy rains 02:20 DW speaks with Yousra Abu Sharekh, aza Program Coordinator with INARA, the International Network for Aid, Relief and Assistance
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Can Korea's Digital Comics Giant Finally Win Over America?
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: Webtoon Entertainment, the company behind the global webcomic phenomenon, pioneered the vertical-scroll format and drew in more than 150 million users worldwide. It completely dominated South Korea and Japan and its partnership with Disney sent its stock soaring 80%. But is it enough to win over the US market? Sohee Kim reports.

item.title: Returning to Syria: Between loss and hope | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Since the toppling of President Bashar al-Assad in December 2024, Syrian refugees and the internally displaced are gradually returning to their homes – and encountering huge challenges in the process. Returning is difficult as many homes have been destroyed, the infrastructure is ailing and the nation’s recovery is being hampered by a severe economic crisis. Last but not least, the country’s political outlook is uncertain. We meet three individuals who have nevertheless taken the courageous decision to go back home: Fatima Zahraa came back to Aleppo to raise her children in a familiar culture, while Amr Yakan gave up a secure life in Canada to rebuild his ancestors’ mosque. After losing his family in the 2023 earthquake, filmmaker Fadi Al-Khatib returned to Damascus in 2023, determined to continue realizing his brother’s dream. Despite the destruction and deprivation, these Syrians choose hope over exile and are rebuilding not only houses, but also their identity. Their stories illustrate the emotional complexity involved in returning to a country still etched by conflict. A reportage by Mohamad Chreyteh.
Chapters: 00:00 Intro 01:10 Fatima returns from Germany 03:37 A new hope for Aleppo? 04:53 Amr feels a sense of duty 07:57 Devastation in Damascus 08:30 A new start for Fadi in Damascus
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews

item.title: Unreported World: Inside Arab communities in Israel with soaring unsolved murder rates
item.author: Krishnan Guru-Murthy
item.content:encoded: Israel? That was my first reaction when they told me I was going there for this series.But of course there is a great deal going on in Israel that is largely unreported and deeply shocking.Just a brief word on terminology: When we speak of Arab-Israelis we are talking mostly about Palestinians, people who would have been citizens of Mandatory Palestine as it was, who are mostly Muslim, with a minority of Christians and Druze as well as Bedouin in areas like the Negev. Many prefer to call themselves Palestinian Citizens of Israel but in East Jerusalem most are not citizens of Israel and do not wish to be, but are resident.Allegations of corruption and negligenceKnowing nods, and brief comments of ‘ah yes, it’s terrible’ came from everyone who asked what we were doing.And many argue this is not just some tragic reality of crime-ridden neighbourhoods. They claim it is a product of deliberate negligence by the far-right Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir who is in charge of the police.Add to that allegations that many of the weapons being used by Arab Israeli criminals are coming from corruption in the police and military.Disparity in murder ‘solved’ ratesThe numbers tell a story straight away: while around 75% of murders are solved in Israel’s Jewish communities, it is around 15% in Arab areas.Arabs make up around 20% of Israel’s population but account for more than 70% of the murder victims, with more than 220 murders so far this year.Arabs make up around 20% of Israel’s population but account for more than 70% of the murder victims, with more than 220 murders so far this year. So what is going on and why is there such a disparity of police success in different communities?An ‘epidemic’ of murders and shootingFirstly, it is clear there is an epidemic of murders and shootings going on in many Arab areas.Many are gang related, with their roots in organised crime, related to extortion of businesses, drugs, guns and car theft.Some follow a tit-for-tat pattern between families – where one killing leads to revenge attacks. And some are just the result of what happens when access to guns is easy for people who want to settle disputes.Almost every day we were filming this story in Israel there was a shooting, stabbing or murder we could have reported on.We focussed on the small coastal town of Jisr as-Zarqa, population around 15,000, where three murders took place in the space of a few days.And it is where the families all had something in common – beyond their grief and insistence that their young men had nothing to do with crime – ask whether the police will solve their case and they almost laugh through the tears.I met Mohamed, whose brother Qusay had been shot dead a couple of days earlier while sitting outside the house next to the falafel kiosk the family owns.On the evening of his killing, the police had stopped and warned him to go inside as they had heard something was going to happen.But they didn’t stay and Qusay didn’t listen – and he was shot by masked gunmen who drove past, all caught on CCTV.Mohamed said he held his brother as he died, waiting for the police and ambulance to arrive, saying he couldn’t breathe.Hopeless task for activistsThere are activists trying to keep young men away from the gangs, trying to teach them traditional skills for a coastal town like fishing and boat skills, practices that historically earned young Palestinian men a living but have largely been lost.But there’s a real sense of them facing a pretty hopeless task – yes, teach a man to fish and he’ll eat for the rest of his life, but teach him to sell drugs or join the gangs and he’ll make enough money to drive around in a flash car and enjoy the good life for as long as he stays alive.The Israeli police were going to give us an interview to discuss the issue, but at the last minute our filming with them was cancelled.It is alluring for some in communities where jobs and opportunities are scarce, and hope in short supply.The Israeli police were going to give us an interview to discuss the issue, but at the last minute our filming with them was cancelled.They insist they are doing their utmost to tackle crime and that Arab Israeli communities don’t always cooperate.But Palestinian politicians tell a different story – believing their communities are being left to descend into chaos on purpose and that many young people are choosing to leave Israel altogether as a result.That, they say, is what Ben-Gvir wants to happen. The Israeli government and Ben-Gvir did not respond to our requests for an interview.Unreported World: Special Substack seriesEach newsletter article – written by our award-winning journalists – will examine and reflect on the extraordinary episodes bringing unique insight.

item.title: Europe Shines on Day 1 of Zagreb Grand Prix
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Europe Shines on Day 1 of Zagreb Grand Prix
The 2025 Zagreb Grand Prix opened with European dominance. Eva Perez Soler, Enzo Jean, Distria Krasniqi, David Garcia Torne and Marica Perisic captured golds as the Arena Zagreb crowd celebrated a thrilling start to the IJF World Tour.

item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Uzbekistan strengthens labour rights through union-led reforms
Uzbekistan’s trade unions mark 120 years of operation, by taking the lead in labour reform, pushing for equal pay, safer workplaces, and stronger employee rights.

item.title: Don't fence me in: US Olympian Ibtihaj Muhammad on getting Muslim girls into sport
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Don't fence me in: US Olympian Ibtihaj Muhammad on getting Muslim girls into sport
US Olympic fencer Ibtihaj Muhammad tells Euronews Culture about her journey and insights on confidence, leadership, and empowerment with young girls in Doha through Qatar Foundation’s Creating Pathways programme.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/11/15/dont-fence-me-in-us-olympian-ibtihaj-muhammad-on-getting-muslim-girls-into-sport
Spark your senses, wake your wonder. Euronews Culture seeks to show creativity in action and inspire our audience to explore the world through the five senses. Start your journey through the best of Europe's arts, gastronomy, traditions and high-end craftsmanship.

item.title: COP30: What to expect from this year's UN climate talks
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
COP30: What to expect from this year's UN climate talks
This year's climate summit is set to spotlight Indigenous peoples, forests and how to adjust course on the Paris Agreement.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/11/10/cop30-what-to-expect-from-this-years-un-climate-talks

item.title: What is the lipstick effect and what does it say about our economy? #economy #shorts
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: What is the lipstick effect and what does it say about our economy? On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin talk about how, in times of economic uncertainty, the beauty industry often thrives as consumers cope by spending on modest luxuries https://link.podtrac.com/ujb7ns2p

item.title: Former Gambian ruler Yahya Jammeh poised to return | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Former Gambian ruler Yahya Jammeh plans his return — sparking fear, hope, and a fight for justice. The government warns he'll be arrested upon his return. But he has many allies and supporters in the West African country. Will he still dare to return?
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1 #gambia #yammeh #africa #dwnewsshorts

item.title: Is Russia luring foreigners to the frontline under false pretenses? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Russia is accused of tricking foreigner civilian into fighting against Ukraine. We meet a man who says he thought he'd secured a job in athletics - only to be sent to the frontlines.
00:00 Ukraine records more foreigners fighting for Russia 00:24 DW Correspondents Max Zander and Felix Maringa report 06:43 DW's Max Zander on conditions in a Ukrainian POW Camp 12:16 DW talks to Robert Goldman, Professor of Law at the American University in Washington DC. He's also Faculty Director of their War Crimes Research Office, and Co-Director of the university's Center For Human Rights and Humanitarian Law.
#ukrainerussiawar #prisonersofwar #russia
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: “If we have a war, nothing else matters” - German voices on the threat NATO faces | Berlin Briefing
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Is Trump’s new Epstein ties investigation ‘an act of hope’ or ‘desperation’? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Donald Trump says he has asked the Justice Department to investigate convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein’s ties to many other high-profile figures. In a post on Truth Social, the US President said he would ask Attorney General Pam Bondi and the FBI to look into Epstein's links to top Democrats -- including former President Bill Clinton. His request follows the release by Congress of emails raising new questions about President Trump's ties to the convicted sex offender. Bondi has confirmed she will launch the investigation on Trump’s orders.
00:00 Trump asks Justice Department to investigate Epstein ties with other high-profile people. 01:04 Michael A. Genovese, Professor of Political Science at Loyola Marymount University in Los Angeles. 05:34 Timothy Hagle is Associate Professor of Political Science at the University of Iowa.
#epsteinfiles #trump #billclinton For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: "Young people are having less sex" - what military service means for gender divide | Berlin Briefing
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Hegseth Unveils Operation Southern Spear | Balance of Power 11/14/2025
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
"Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Betsey Stevenson, University of Michigan Public Policy & Economics Professor, talks about President Trump signing tariff relief on some groceries. Executive Director of Arizona State University's McCain Institute, Evelyn Farkas, shares her thoughts on whether or not the US is preparing to enter a war with Venezuela. Ty Cobb, former prosecutor & former special counsel to President Trump during the first administration, discusses President Trump asking the DOJ and FBI to investigate other prominent Americans on ties to Jeffrey Epstein.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Balance Power: Late Edition 00:02:29 - Lauren Dezenski Talks What to Make of White House Announcing Tariff Reductions on Beef, Coffee, Bananas, Etc. 00:05:51 - Betsey Stevenson, Univ. of Michigan Public Policy & Eco. Prof., on Economic Outlook Of Trump Tariff Exemptions, When Will Exemptions Be Felt 00:12:40 - Power Moves: Stocks Make Cautious Comeback as Fed Angst Lingers 00:17:18 - Evelyn Farkas, Executive Dir. at Arizona State Univ. McCain Institute, On Is US Potentially Preparing Strikes With Venezuela 00:24:59 - Ty Cobb, Fmr. Federal Prosecutor, on Pres. Trump Asking DOJ & FBI to Investigate Other Prominent Americans on Ties to Epstein 00:34:46 - Antonia Mufarech on Understanding Chilean Presidential Election 00:38:00 - Power Brief: Top Stories Watching Tonight 00:39:37 - Political Panel React to House Will Vote to Undo Senate Phone Records Provisions, Trump-Epstein Ties Back in Spotlight, House to Vote on Epstein Files -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Stockholm bus crash: Several killed and others injured | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Several people have been killed and others injured, after a double-decker bus hit a bus stop in the Swedish capital, Stockholm during Friday evening rush-hour. Local media reports say the driver has been arrested. Police say they do not suspect the incident to be terror-related.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1 #stockholm #buscrash #police #dwnewsshorts

item.title: Is Russia stockpiling weapons to overwhelm Ukraine’s defense at one go? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
On Thursday night-Friday morning, Russia launched a massive overnight attack on Kyiv, using hundreds of drones and at least eighteen missiles. Multiple residential buildings across the Ukrainian capital were hit, with emergency services reporting multiple deaths and injuries. Ukrainian authorities say this is the biggest strike on Kyiv in almost three weeks, and repeated their calls to allies to supply more air-defence systems.
00:00 Russia strikes Kyiv in large-scale overnight attack 03:11 Military analyst Marina Miron from King's College London. 11:05 Domitilla Sagramoso, Senior Lecturer in Security and Development at King's College London. 15:08 DW correspondent Nick Connolly
#ukrainewar #russia #kyiv For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Stocks Make Cautious Comeback | The Close 11/14/2025
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Wilmington Trust's Tony Roth, BNY Investments’ Stephanie Pierce, Morgan Stanley’s Joseph Moore, Sperling Economic’s Gene Sperling, Wells Fargo Investment Institute’s Veronica Willis, Morningstar’s Jack Shannon, Commonwealth Edison’s Gil Quiniones, Jamba Juice’s James D. White, It’s A 10 Haircare’s Carolyn Aronson, Knight Commission’s Amy Privette Perko.
Chapters: 00:00:00 - Bloomberg Markets: The Close 00:03:43 - Wilmington Trust's Tony Roth 00:10:06 - BNY Investments’ Stephanie Pierce 00:16:15 - Top Calls 00:17:39 - Morgan Stanley’s Joseph Moore 00:24:08 - Sperling Economic’s Gene Sperling 00:33:37 - Norah Mulinda, Bloomberg News 00:36:42 - Wells Fargo Investment Institute’s Veronica Willis 00:51:39 - Julie Fine, Bloomberg News 00:56:07 - Morningstar’s Jack Shannon 01:02:45 - Commonwealth Edison’s Gil Quiniones 01:09:37 - Jamba Juice’s James D. White 01:16:08 - It’s A 10 Haircare’s Carolyn Aronson 01:22:18 - Knight Commission’s Amy Privette Perko 01:27:42 - Hema Parmar, Bloomberg News -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Regulation Is Holding Back Europe’s Banks, Santander’s Botín Says
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Santander Executive Chair Ana Botín discusses the bank’s record year, but cautions that Europe’s regulatory burden is holding back growth. Botín explains how Santander’s global expansion gives it an edge, even as European banks struggle with slower reform and higher taxes. She is a longtime proponent of capital markets integration in Europe, but even she isn’t confident that it will happen any time soon. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Nothing Can Distract From Trump-Epstein Ties: Cobb
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Ty Cobb, former prosecutor & former special counsel to President Trump during the first administration, discusses President Trump asking the DOJ and FBI to investigate other prominent Americans on ties to Jeffrey Epstein. Cobb talks about how many investigations there could be, where this is headed, and states these investigations will be to overwhelm and distract the evidence against the President. Ty Cobb speaks with Joe Mathieu and Tyler Kendall on the late edition of Bloomberg's "Balance of Power." -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Fridays for Future not what it used to be | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Fridays for Future rallies took place in around 70 cities across Germany, but the youth activist movement appears to no longer be mobilizing as many people as it used to. Activists say the political agenda has switched focus - and so has the public's.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews
Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1 #fridaysforfuture #environment #youngpeople

item.title: Bentley CEO unveils the Supersports #shorts #cars #bentley #supersports
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: Fresh off the global debut of Bentley’s brand new reveal: The Continental GT Supersports. It's a lighter, louder, rear-wheel-drive bruiser, and Bentley CEO Frank-Steffen Walliser joins Matt Miller to discuss http://bit.ly/3XwKcYE

item.title: Why has the US put German group Antifa-Ost on a terror list that includes Hamas and ISIS? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
The United States has announced it is declaring four groups based in Europe as "foreign terrorist organizations". They include one group from Germany, called Antifa Ost. The other groups are from Italy and Greece. 00:00 DW Correspondent Matthew Moore reports
#rubio #antiterrorist
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
Follow DW on social media: ►Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dwnews ►TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@dwnews ►Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/ ►Twitter: https://twitter.com/dwnews

item.title: #russsia's Putin is not ready to make a deal, former MI6 Chief Says
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Intel shows that Vladimir Putin is not ready to make a deal to end the war in Ukraine, former MI6 chief Richard Moore tells Mishal Husain. Subscribe to The Mishal Husain Show wherever you get your podcasts https://t.co/TooEnnzwQL
You can find the written version of this interview with Mishal’s notes on Bloomberg Weekend: www.bloomberg.com/latest/weekend-interview
Contact The Mishal Husain Show mishalshow@bloomberg.net
Visit https://www.bloomberg.com/mishal Watch Bloomberg Vodcasts: • Bloomberg Vodcasts | Video Podcasts, Inter...
Subscribe to Bloomberg Podcasts: https://bit.ly/BloombergPodcasts
Visit us: https://www.bloomberg.com/podcasts
Visit our other YouTube channels: Bloomberg Television: / @markets Bloomberg Originals: / bloomberg
For coverage on news, markets and more: http://www.bloomberg.com/video

item.title: Is The United States Potentially Preparing to Enter a War With Venezuela?
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Executive Director of Arizona State University's McCain Institute, Evelyn Farka, shares her thoughts on whether or not the US is preparing to enter a war with Venezuela as the USS Gerald R. Ford is in the region & the US continues to strike boats allegedly smuggling drugs from Venezuela. Farkas states these actions are about regime change and talks about if these strikes will scare & intimidate Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro out of office. She speaks with Joe Mathieu and Tyler Kendall on the late edition of Bloomberg's "Balance of Power." -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: How the Netherlands is addressing the retirement crisis#shorts #retirement #pension
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: Around the world, market forces – low interest rates, longer lives, workers changing jobs – are testing underfunded pension plans. On tonight's "Wall Street Week," we explore how the Netherlands is rethinking financial security for aging populations http://bloom.bg/4peg1kV

item.title: Heavy rain swamps Gaza City tent camp | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Heavy rain in Gaza City flooded a tent camp overnight, soaking displaced residents and their belongings. Some 90% of Gazans are displaced after Israeli attacks destroyed most of the enclave's housing.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
